channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Ticket Scam Cost the Louvre $12 Million, Investigators Say
item.description: The Paris prosecutor’s office said that a network involving museum employees and tour guides had been operating for a decade. Investigators also belie...

item.title: How a Trump Tax Break Rescued Horse Racing
item.description: Owners spent nearly $1.5 billion last year on racehorses, a big increase over 2024. A new tax provision allows them to immediately deduct the full cos...

item.title: 5 New Dumpling Recipes for Lunar New Year 2026
item.description: Our annual tribute to one of everyone’s favorite foods returns, with all-new ways to get you pinching and pleating.

item.title: In Hungary, Marco Rubio Stresses Trump’s Support for Viktor Orban
item.description: The U.S. secretary of state said in Budapest that the president was “deeply committed” to the success of Viktor Orban, who has boasted of creating an ...

item.title: One Olympic sport doesn't allow women. These Games could determine its future
item.description: Nordic combined is the only Olympic sport that doesn't allow women to compete, despite athletes' efforts to change that. They say their odds for 2030 ...

item.title: How to register to vote in the 2026 primaries
item.description: For the 2026 primary elections, NPR has collected deadlines and information on how to register to vote — online, in person or by mail — in every U.S. ...

item.title: U.S. Olympic speed skaters adapt NASCAR 'bump drafting,' revolutionizing team event
item.description: U.S. Team Pursuit speed skaters will top speeds of 30 mph by pushing themselves around the track mere inches from each other.

item.title: 'American Struggle' author assesses Trump's expansion of presidential power
item.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning historian Jon Meacham talks about Trump's impact on democracy. Meacham's latest book is a collection of speeches, letters and o...

item.title: DHS shuts down after a funding lapse. And, why athletes get the 'yips' at the Olympics
item.description: Congress is out on recess as a partial shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security is underway. And, why some superstar athletes have been getting...

item.title: Trial Begins for Father of Teen Charged in Georgia School Shooting
item.description: Prosecutors say Colin Gray is criminally culpable after his teenage son killed two students and two teachers with a rifle that had been a Christmas gi...

item.title: U.S. Deports Nine Migrants in Secret, Ignoring Legal Protections
item.description: Most had court orders protecting them from removal to their home countries, so they were sent to detention in Cameroon.

item.title: Iran’s Protests Have Ended, but the Anger and Pain Haven’t
item.description: In the aftermath of another wave of antigovernment unrest, Iran is gripped by a mood of collective grief and uncertainty about the future.

item.title: Cloudflare Reports Issues as Users Report Problems With Multiple Sites
item.description: Cloudflare, the tech company that provides services for a wide range of apps and websites, said it was working to fix an internal issue.

item.title: Ticket Scam Cost the Louvre $12 Million, Investigators Say
item.description: The Paris prosecutor’s office said that a network involving museum employees and tour guides had been operating for a decade. Investigators also belie...

item.title: In Hungary, Marco Rubio Stresses Trump’s Support for Viktor Orban
item.description: The U.S. secretary of state said in Budapest that the president was “deeply committed” to the success of Viktor Orban, who has boasted of creating an ...
